revision.comment: Bot: created missing page [[Talk:Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine]] redirect to enwiki [[Talk:Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine]]

imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Bot: remove deleted category Automatic wikipedia redirects
Bot: migrate talk page structure; import discussion seed (ja:鳥海山大物忌神社, en:Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine); add local discussion section + dated import note
revision.text:
{{talk page header}}
<!-- This talk page covers the main article and all associated namespace layers --> <!-- Imported from Japanese/English/Simple English Wikipedia talk pages on 2026-02-26 (UTC) when available. -->
== Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:00, 26 February 2026 (UTC) <!-- Add local discussion below this line. -->
== Imported from Japanese Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/29943729 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:00, 26 February 2026 (UTC) <!-- Source: ja:Talk:鳥海山大物忌神社 | revid=29943729 --> == 鳥海修験への分割提案について == 当所、スタブ程度の記事しかなかったことから、本記事の1セクションとして記載した[[鳥海修験]]ですが、以下の理由により分割をご提案致します。 #記事が[[鳥海山大物忌神社]]のみならず、[[鳥海山]]周辺の複数の神社・寺院に関連した広範囲な内容になってきていること。 #複数の神社・寺院に関連した内容でありながら、鳥海山大物忌神社の記事中に占める割合が大きくなってきており、今後も追記が予想されること。 特に異論が無ければ、[[平成]]22年（[[2010年]]）1月10日付けで鳥海修験のセクションを新しい記事として分割したいと思います。分割が不要との意見が多ければ、このまま鳥海山大物忌神社の記事中で、鳥海修験に関する追記を続けます。皆様のご意見をお願い申し上げます。 --[[利用者:ひら室長|ひら室長]] 2009年12月30日 (水) 15:37 (UTC)
*{{賛成}} 異議無し。（ついでですが、分割が施行された場合には、[[Wikipedia:分割提案]]へのご報告をお願い致します。）--[[利用者:胡亂堂|胡亂堂]] 2009年12月31日 (木) 02:17 (UTC) *{{賛成}} 私も異議ありません。文化財指定の関係上、追記も見込まれますしね。--[[利用者:Law soma|ろう(Law soma)]] <small><sup>[[利用者‐会話:Law soma|D]]</sup> <sub>[[特別:Contributions/Law soma|C]]</sub></small> 2010年1月4日 (月) 04:55 (UTC)
{{報告}} ご賛同頂きまして、ありがとうございます。それでは他に異議もありませんので、鳥海修験へ分割を行います。--[[利用者:ひら室長|ひら室長]] 2010年1月10日 (日) 11:40 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:00, 26 February 2026 (UTC)
== Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1206672665 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:00, 26 February 2026 (UTC) <!-- Source: en:Talk:Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine | revid=1206672665 --> {{WikiProject banner shell|class=C|1= {{WikiProject Articles for creation|ts=20231112194639|reviewer=Turnagra|oldid=1184690372}} {{WikiProject Japan|shinto=yes}} {{WikiProject Architecture}} {{WikiProject Religion}} {{WikiProject Religious Buildings, Architecture and Monasticism}} }}
== Article prose ==
@[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] I thought it would be better to comment here rather than in the AfC comments. I think it still needs a bit of work, I'm afraid. My main issue with the prose is that you've got a lot of standalone, single sentence paragraphs which make the article feel more like a list of bullet points than an actual article. What I'd suggest would be to check out the articles of other, more famous shrines or temples to get a feel for what sort of stuff they cover, and how they do so - for example, [[Kiyomizu-dera]] or [[Itsukushima Shrine]]. [[User:Turnagra|Turnagra]] ([[User talk:Turnagra|talk]]) 07:52, 11 November 2023 (UTC)
:@[[User:Turnagra|Turnagra]] sorry for tagging you a bit too soon but I organized it just a bit more in a way that I think made my initial intentions a bit clearer. Do you see it as an improvement? [[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 07:59, 11 November 2023 (UTC) ::@[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] it's definitely improving, but I think there is still a wee way to go yet. [[User:Turnagra|Turnagra]] ([[User talk:Turnagra|talk]]) 08:09, 11 November 2023 (UTC) :::@[[User:Turnagra|Turnagra]] I think I managed to figure out what you wanted. A bit of a harder writing style but it is a lot more pleasant to read now I think. [[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 08:47, 11 November 2023 (UTC) ::::@[[User:Turnagra|Turnagra]] did you get to read it yet? [[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 21:49, 11 November 2023 (UTC) :::::@[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] Sorry, your last ping was just after I'd logged off for the night. What you've got now is absolutely a massive improvement and it's looking a lot better, good job! I think the article could still do with some further explanation for people unfamiliar with the shrine and shintoism. For example: :::::* What does it mean that Omonoimi no Kami ranks up? :::::* What was the outcome of the legal dispute after WW2? :::::* What is the Hi-awase shinji festival? You've got a bit about it but I'd love to know more. :::::* Do the history of the sub-shrines differ from the history of the shrine as a whole? It could be good to get some expansion of their respective histories and anything interesting about them. :::::These aren't necessarily required for the article to be accepted but would be a huge help to the overall quality of the article. What it would need still is I think some more work on the copy and a solid proof - make sure things roughly make sense and flow nicely, and any typos are picked up. [[User:Turnagra|Turnagra]] ([[User talk:Turnagra|talk]]) 23:14, 11 November 2023 (UTC) ::::::@[[User:Turnagra|Turnagra]] I tried fixing some of it. I definitely managed to expand on the ranking up and the festival, but I'm not actually sure what the outcome of the legal dispute was. As for the local histories likewise research is a bit hard on it. I think I reached my limit on those two things and will need a Japanese speaking collaborator for more info on those two things. Anything else you think could be improved? [[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 23:48, 11 November 2023 (UTC) :::::::Thanks, it's definitely shaping up well - I've made a few edits to the lede as well to try a bit more. My last thing at this stage is that I'm still a bit iffy about some of the sources, particularly what are now sources 5 and 6. What makes these reliable? [[User:Turnagra|Turnagra]] ([[User talk:Turnagra|talk]]) 01:18, 12 November 2023 (UTC) ::::::::@[[User:Turnagra|Turnagra]] I believe the person who wrote 5 has an educational certification from the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] but I will have to do a bit more digging to be 100% sure on that. I hope to get back to you tomorrow confident on it ::::::::As for 6 it is more questionable and I might want to get better sources for the claims. ::::::::I can confidently say I do not consider 5 to be a tourist site but 6 seems to be one. [[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 07:06, 12 November 2023 (UTC) :::::::::@[[User:Turnagra|Turnagra]] the [https://twitter.com/tonbo_shrine creator of the website] has this certification https://www.jinjakentei.jp/kenteitoha.html {{nihongo|first-class Shinto shrine certification|神社検定壱級|Jinja Kentei Ikkkyū}} which has involvement from [[Kokugakuin University]] and [[Kogakkan University]]. But I am unsure about what the nature of the certification is, although they appear to be some kind of lecturer now. [[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 19:04, 12 November 2023 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:00, 26 February 2026 (UTC)

{{talk page header}}
<!-- This talk page covers the main article and all associated namespace layers --> <!-- Imported from Japanese/English/Simple English Wikipedia talk pages on 2026-02-26 (UTC) when available. -->
== Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:00, 26 February 2026 (UTC) <!-- Add local discussion below this line. -->
== Imported from Japanese Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/29943729 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:00, 26 February 2026 (UTC) <!-- Source: ja:Talk:鳥海山大物忌神社 | revid=29943729 --> == 鳥海修験への分割提案について == 当所、スタブ程度の記事しかなかったことから、本記事の1セクションとして記載した[[鳥海修験]]ですが、以下の理由により分割をご提案致します。 #記事が[[鳥海山大物忌神社]]のみならず、[[鳥海山]]周辺の複数の神社・寺院に関連した広範囲な内容になってきていること。 #複数の神社・寺院に関連した内容でありながら、鳥海山大物忌神社の記事中に占める割合が大きくなってきており、今後も追記が予想されること。 特に異論が無ければ、[[平成]]22年（[[2010年]]）1月10日付けで鳥海修験のセクションを新しい記事として分割したいと思います。分割が不要との意見が多ければ、このまま鳥海山大物忌神社の記事中で、鳥海修験に関する追記を続けます。皆様のご意見をお願い申し上げます。 --[[User:ひら室長|ひら室長]] 2009年12月30日 (水) 15:37 (UTC)
*{{賛成}} 異議無し。（ついでですが、分割が施行された場合には、[[Wikipedia:分割提案]]へのご報告をお願い致します。）--[[User:胡亂堂|胡亂堂]] 2009年12月31日 (木) 02:17 (UTC) *{{賛成}} 私も異議ありません。文化財指定の関係上、追記も見込まれますしね。--[[User:Law soma|ろう(Law soma)]] <small><sup>[[利用者‐会話:Law soma|D]]</sup> <sub>[[特別:Contributions/Law soma|C]]</sub></small> 2010年1月4日 (月) 04:55 (UTC)
{{報告}} ご賛同頂きまして、ありがとうございます。それでは他に異議もありませんので、鳥海修験へ分割を行います。--[[User:ひら室長|ひら室長]] 2010年1月10日 (日) 11:40 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:00, 26 February 2026 (UTC)
== Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1206672665 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:00, 26 February 2026 (UTC) <!-- Source: en:Talk:Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine | revid=1206672665 --> {{WikiProject banner shell|class=C|1= {{WikiProject Articles for creation|ts=20231112194639|reviewer=Turnagra|oldid=1184690372}} {{WikiProject Japan|shinto=yes}} {{WikiProject Architecture}} {{WikiProject Religion}} {{WikiProject Religious Buildings, Architecture and Monasticism}} }}
== Article prose ==
@[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] I thought it would be better to comment here rather than in the AfC comments. I think it still needs a bit of work, I'm afraid. My main issue with the prose is that you've got a lot of standalone, single sentence paragraphs which make the article feel more like a list of bullet points than an actual article. What I'd suggest would be to check out the articles of other, more famous shrines or temples to get a feel for what sort of stuff they cover, and how they do so - for example, [[Kiyomizu-dera]] or [[Itsukushima Shrine]]. [[User:Turnagra|Turnagra]] ([[User talk:Turnagra|talk]]) 07:52, 11 November 2023 (UTC)
:@[[User:Turnagra|Turnagra]] sorry for tagging you a bit too soon but I organized it just a bit more in a way that I think made my initial intentions a bit clearer. Do you see it as an improvement? [[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 07:59, 11 November 2023 (UTC) ::@[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] it's definitely improving, but I think there is still a wee way to go yet. [[User:Turnagra|Turnagra]] ([[User talk:Turnagra|talk]]) 08:09, 11 November 2023 (UTC) :::@[[User:Turnagra|Turnagra]] I think I managed to figure out what you wanted. A bit of a harder writing style but it is a lot more pleasant to read now I think. [[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 08:47, 11 November 2023 (UTC) ::::@[[User:Turnagra|Turnagra]] did you get to read it yet? [[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 21:49, 11 November 2023 (UTC) :::::@[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] Sorry, your last ping was just after I'd logged off for the night. What you've got now is absolutely a massive improvement and it's looking a lot better, good job! I think the article could still do with some further explanation for people unfamiliar with the shrine and shintoism. For example: :::::* What does it mean that Omonoimi no Kami ranks up? :::::* What was the outcome of the legal dispute after WW2? :::::* What is the Hi-awase shinji festival? You've got a bit about it but I'd love to know more. :::::* Do the history of the sub-shrines differ from the history of the shrine as a whole? It could be good to get some expansion of their respective histories and anything interesting about them. :::::These aren't necessarily required for the article to be accepted but would be a huge help to the overall quality of the article. What it would need still is I think some more work on the copy and a solid proof - make sure things roughly make sense and flow nicely, and any typos are picked up. [[User:Turnagra|Turnagra]] ([[User talk:Turnagra|talk]]) 23:14, 11 November 2023 (UTC) ::::::@[[User:Turnagra|Turnagra]] I tried fixing some of it. I definitely managed to expand on the ranking up and the festival, but I'm not actually sure what the outcome of the legal dispute was. As for the local histories likewise research is a bit hard on it. I think I reached my limit on those two things and will need a Japanese speaking collaborator for more info on those two things. Anything else you think could be improved? [[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 23:48, 11 November 2023 (UTC) :::::::Thanks, it's definitely shaping up well - I've made a few edits to the lede as well to try a bit more. My last thing at this stage is that I'm still a bit iffy about some of the sources, particularly what are now sources 5 and 6. What makes these reliable? [[User:Turnagra|Turnagra]] ([[User talk:Turnagra|talk]]) 01:18, 12 November 2023 (UTC) ::::::::@[[User:Turnagra|Turnagra]] I believe the person who wrote 5 has an educational certification from the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] but I will have to do a bit more digging to be 100% sure on that. I hope to get back to you tomorrow confident on it ::::::::As for 6 it is more questionable and I might want to get better sources for the claims. ::::::::I can confidently say I do not consider 5 to be a tourist site but 6 seems to be one. [[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 07:06, 12 November 2023 (UTC) :::::::::@[[User:Turnagra|Turnagra]] the [https://twitter.com/tonbo_shrine creator of the website] has this certification https://www.jinjakentei.jp/kenteitoha.html {{nihongo|first-class Shinto shrine certification|神社検定壱級|Jinja Kentei Ikkkyū}} which has involvement from [[Kokugakuin University]] and [[Kogakkan University]]. But I am unsure about what the nature of the certification is, although they appear to be some kind of lecturer now. [[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 19:04, 12 November 2023 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:00, 26 February 2026 (UTC)

revision.comment: [[Project:History cleanup|Offloading history due to miraheze stability concerns]] many contributors, see [https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Ch%C5%8Dkaisan_%C5%8Cmonoimi_Shrine here] for full history [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!-- History offloaded: due to miraheze stability concerns, the edit history of this page has been offloaded to the XML archive. See https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Ch%C5%8Dkaisan_%C5%8Cmonoimi_Shrine for full history. Last refreshed: [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]] --> {{talk page header}}
<!-- This talk page covers the main article and all associated namespace layers --> <!-- Imported from Japanese/English/Simple English Wikipedia talk pages on 2026-02-26 (UTC) when available. -->
== Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:00, 26 February 2026 (UTC) <!-- Add local discussion below this line. -->
== Imported from Japanese Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/29943729 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:00, 26 February 2026 (UTC) <!-- Source: ja:Talk:鳥海山大物忌神社 | revid=29943729 --> == 鳥海修験への分割提案について == 当所、スタブ程度の記事しかなかったことから、本記事の1セクションとして記載した[[鳥海修験]]ですが、以下の理由により分割をご提案致します。 #記事が[[鳥海山大物忌神社]]のみならず、[[鳥海山]]周辺の複数の神社・寺院に関連した広範囲な内容になってきていること。 #複数の神社・寺院に関連した内容でありながら、鳥海山大物忌神社の記事中に占める割合が大きくなってきており、今後も追記が予想されること。 特に異論が無ければ、[[平成]]22年（[[2010年]]）1月10日付けで鳥海修験のセクションを新しい記事として分割したいと思います。分割が不要との意見が多ければ、このまま鳥海山大物忌神社の記事中で、鳥海修験に関する追記を続けます。皆様のご意見をお願い申し上げます。 --[[User:ひら室長|ひら室長]] 2009年12月30日 (水) 15:37 (UTC)
*{{賛成}} 異議無し。（ついでですが、分割が施行された場合には、[[Wikipedia:分割提案]]へのご報告をお願い致します。）--[[User:胡亂堂|胡亂堂]] 2009年12月31日 (木) 02:17 (UTC) *{{賛成}} 私も異議ありません。文化財指定の関係上、追記も見込まれますしね。--[[User:Law soma|ろう(Law soma)]] <small><sup>[[利用者‐会話:Law soma|D]]</sup> <sub>[[特別:Contributions/Law soma|C]]</sub></small> 2010年1月4日 (月) 04:55 (UTC)
{{報告}} ご賛同頂きまして、ありがとうございます。それでは他に異議もありませんので、鳥海修験へ分割を行います。--[[User:ひら室長|ひら室長]] 2010年1月10日 (日) 11:40 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:00, 26 February 2026 (UTC)
== Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1206672665 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:00, 26 February 2026 (UTC) <!-- Source: en:Talk:Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine | revid=1206672665 --> {{WikiProject banner shell|class=C|1= {{WikiProject Articles for creation|ts=20231112194639|reviewer=Turnagra|oldid=1184690372}} {{WikiProject Japan|shinto=yes}} {{WikiProject Architecture}} {{WikiProject Religion}} {{WikiProject Religious Buildings, Architecture and Monasticism}} }}
== Article prose ==
@[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] I thought it would be better to comment here rather than in the AfC comments. I think it still needs a bit of work, I'm afraid. My main issue with the prose is that you've got a lot of standalone, single sentence paragraphs which make the article feel more like a list of bullet points than an actual article. What I'd suggest would be to check out the articles of other, more famous shrines or temples to get a feel for what sort of stuff they cover, and how they do so - for example, [[Kiyomizu-dera]] or [[Itsukushima Shrine]]. [[User:Turnagra|Turnagra]] ([[User talk:Turnagra|talk]]) 07:52, 11 November 2023 (UTC)
:@[[User:Turnagra|Turnagra]] sorry for tagging you a bit too soon but I organized it just a bit more in a way that I think made my initial intentions a bit clearer. Do you see it as an improvement? [[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 07:59, 11 November 2023 (UTC) ::@[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] it's definitely improving, but I think there is still a wee way to go yet. [[User:Turnagra|Turnagra]] ([[User talk:Turnagra|talk]]) 08:09, 11 November 2023 (UTC) :::@[[User:Turnagra|Turnagra]] I think I managed to figure out what you wanted. A bit of a harder writing style but it is a lot more pleasant to read now I think. [[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 08:47, 11 November 2023 (UTC) ::::@[[User:Turnagra|Turnagra]] did you get to read it yet? [[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 21:49, 11 November 2023 (UTC) :::::@[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] Sorry, your last ping was just after I'd logged off for the night. What you've got now is absolutely a massive improvement and it's looking a lot better, good job! I think the article could still do with some further explanation for people unfamiliar with the shrine and shintoism. For example: :::::* What does it mean that Omonoimi no Kami ranks up? :::::* What was the outcome of the legal dispute after WW2? :::::* What is the Hi-awase shinji festival? You've got a bit about it but I'd love to know more. :::::* Do the history of the sub-shrines differ from the history of the shrine as a whole? It could be good to get some expansion of their respective histories and anything interesting about them. :::::These aren't necessarily required for the article to be accepted but would be a huge help to the overall quality of the article. What it would need still is I think some more work on the copy and a solid proof - make sure things roughly make sense and flow nicely, and any typos are picked up. [[User:Turnagra|Turnagra]] ([[User talk:Turnagra|talk]]) 23:14, 11 November 2023 (UTC) ::::::@[[User:Turnagra|Turnagra]] I tried fixing some of it. I definitely managed to expand on the ranking up and the festival, but I'm not actually sure what the outcome of the legal dispute was. As for the local histories likewise research is a bit hard on it. I think I reached my limit on those two things and will need a Japanese speaking collaborator for more info on those two things. Anything else you think could be improved? [[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 23:48, 11 November 2023 (UTC) :::::::Thanks, it's definitely shaping up well - I've made a few edits to the lede as well to try a bit more. My last thing at this stage is that I'm still a bit iffy about some of the sources, particularly what are now sources 5 and 6. What makes these reliable? [[User:Turnagra|Turnagra]] ([[User talk:Turnagra|talk]]) 01:18, 12 November 2023 (UTC) ::::::::@[[User:Turnagra|Turnagra]] I believe the person who wrote 5 has an educational certification from the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] but I will have to do a bit more digging to be 100% sure on that. I hope to get back to you tomorrow confident on it ::::::::As for 6 it is more questionable and I might want to get better sources for the claims. ::::::::I can confidently say I do not consider 5 to be a tourist site but 6 seems to be one. [[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 07:06, 12 November 2023 (UTC) :::::::::@[[User:Turnagra|Turnagra]] the [https://twitter.com/tonbo_shrine creator of the website] has this certification https://www.jinjakentei.jp/kenteitoha.html {{nihongo|first-class Shinto shrine certification|神社検定壱級|Jinja Kentei Ikkkyū}} which has involvement from [[Kokugakuin University]] and [[Kogakkan University]]. But I am unsure about what the nature of the certification is, although they appear to be some kind of lecturer now. [[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 19:04, 12 November 2023 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:00, 26 February 2026 (UTC)
